summaries.121868: The issue proved difficult to reproduce: one triage member could not reproduce on iPad Air M1 with 2nd gen Apple Pencil, and another could not reproduce on iPad Pro with 1st gen Pencil on iOS 16.1.1 or 16.3.1. The original reporter confirmed the bug persisted on master on an iPad Air 3rd gen (non-M1) with 1st gen Pencil, suggesting a device-specific factor. A later comment identified a new related variant where using `supportedLocales` breaks Scribble with `maxLines: 1`, while using `locale` directly works; emoji also fail with a console error `[PencilTextInput] ResultCommand: Could not process result replacing range because it is out of bounds`, pointing to a possible extended grapheme cluster handling bug in the Scribble PRs (#97437, engine #24224). A final comment from the reporter on 3.19.9 stated the original issue is fixed.
summaries.139443: Comments confirmed reproduction on Windows with RTL (Persian/Farsi) multiline text that was manually typed (not pasted); pasting the same text works correctly. The root cause was identified as a caret placement issue: when text contains CRLF-style line endings from manual typing, the cursor offset reported by the framework is incorrect for RTL text in multiline mode on Windows. A commenter linked this to issue #140739 and suspected a connection to CRLF handling. Another commenter noted the bug also manifests with custom fonts on Android (wrong caret position and incorrect word selection). A user shared a workaround that manually inserts `\r\n` instead of `\n` on Enter. The assignee (LongCatIsLooong) was removed after no status updates.
summaries.101196: Comments confirmed the clipboard-reading alert is reproducible on Xiaomi MIUI 12.5 (public dev version) and affects Flutter 3.16.6, with the stack trace pointing to `getPrimaryClipDescription` inside `PlatformPlugin.clipboardHasStrings` being called dozens of times per second. The root cause is that even though `hasPrimaryClip`/`getPrimaryClipDescription` was implemented (engine #27215, framework #87678) to avoid reading clipboard contents, some Xiaomi/MIUI devices still trigger the security alert for those calls. A workaround was proposed (always return `ClipboardStatus.pasteable`) and a PR was filed (#179840) to reduce the loop calls from `didUpdateWidget`. The team discussed whether to always show the Paste button on Android as an alternative but no fix has landed.
summaries.94441: Comments confirmed reproduction on both stable and master channels on Android with a Bluetooth keyboard. The root cause is that after alt+tab switches away and back to the app, the engine synthesizes a key-up event for Alt, but when a new key-repeat event arrives the framework asserts `_pressedKeys.containsKey` fails because the key was never recorded as re-pressed, leaving the keyboard in an unusable state. Non-Flutter apps do not exhibit this behavior. The issue was escalated to the framework team (@dkwingsmt, @HansMuller, @gspencergoog, @LongCatIsLooong) and remained open with no fix.
summaries.120633: Comments clarified that the bug manifests only when the TextField is empty — the `OutlineInputBorder` does not expand to fill the available height given by an `Expanded` widget. A workaround was identified: wrap the `TextField` in `SizedBox.expand`. A commenter also noted possible interaction with the `expands` parameter (introduced in #27205) and that clicking below the border incorrectly focuses the field, which is considered a separate sub-bug. No fix has been implemented.
summaries.155073: Comments established that the spacebar failure is intermittent, occurs only in release/production mode (not debug), affects MS Edge but not Chrome, and is linked to a separate focus-regression issue (#59439) — when #59439 triggers, this spacebar issue also triggers, and an F5 soft-reload fixes both. The issue also affects Enter key (`onFieldSubmitted` does not fire). One commenter identified that removing the `SingleActivator(LogicalKeyboardKey.space)` shortcut from `_defaultWebShortcuts` in `app.dart` fixes it, and another resolved it by regenerating `index.html` via `flutter create . --platforms web`, suggesting a stale custom bootstrapping script in `index.html` is a contributing factor. Currently open with no upstream fix.
summaries.133537: Comments confirmed the regression is iOS-specific (not reproducible on Android, where the keyboard is dismissed before the image picker appears). The bug occurs only when dismissing the image picker via Cancel or Add buttons — dismissing via pull-down gesture does not trigger it. Reproduction was confirmed on iPhone 7 (iOS 15.7.2), iPhone 13 Pro Max, and SE; not reproducible on iPhone 14 Pro simulator. The root cause was bisected to commit 9aa2ea150c and attributed to engine PR #37719. `MediaQuery.viewInsets.bottom` shows a different value (233px vs 260px) after the buggy dismissal. Setting priority to P1 as a regression. A workaround is to call `focusNode.unfocus()` before opening the picker and then request focus again afterward.
summaries.97253: Comments confirmed the bug is reproducible on both Android and iOS on stable (3.29.3) and master (3.32.0-1.0.pre.514). The `TextInputConnection.updateConfig` method sends the updated configuration to the platform but the soft keyboard's action button does not visually update while the keyboard is already open. No root cause beyond the method not triggering a keyboard reload was identified. No PR or fix exists; status is open.
summaries.168781: The root cause was identified from the stack trace: when the user scrolls outside the TextField (triggering `onTapOutside` → `focusNode.unfocus()`), the keyboard dismisses and the scroll view's dimensions change, which causes `ScrollPositionWithSingleContext.goBallistic` → `ScrollDragController.cancel` → `_StretchController._recede` → `AnimationController.forward` to fire a `setState` during layout, triggering the "Build scheduled during frame" assertion. Affects Android (confirmed on Pixel 2 and Samsung Galaxy S24 with Gboard and Samsung Keyboard). A workaround is to add `AlwaysScrollableScrollPhysics()` to the `SingleChildScrollView`. The framework team acknowledged this is a scrolling framework bug where `ScrollableState.setCanDrag` should not trigger a rebuild during layout.
summaries.126853: Comments identified that the bug only triggers when navigating away from a page while a TextField still has focus (keyboard not dismissed) — pressing the keyboard Done button before swiping prevents the issue. Confirmed on Android (Samsung Galaxy S22/S9/Note II, Nokia G21, Android emulators) and iOS (iPhone 7, SE 3rd gen, iOS simulator). Two workarounds exist: (1) call `FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus()` in `onPageChanged`, or (2) assign a distinct `TextEditingController` to each page's TextField. The root cause is Flutter reusing widget state across PageView pages when focus is retained, causing the text state of the old TextField to leak into the new page's widget.
summaries.128565: Comments established that `isComposingRangeValid` behavior is inconsistent across platforms: on iOS it correctly returns `true` only during CJK composition and `false` after candidate confirmation, but on Android it behaves differently depending on whether the user types on the keyboard grid vs. selects from the suggestion bar. On Android with Gboard, typing characters returns `true` even for English, while selecting suggested text returns `false`. A commenter confirmed this is a regression introduced in 3.10.1–3.10.5 specifically for Apple and Google CJK keyboards. A PR (#130081) was filed to address this. A workaround using `WidgetsBinding.instance.addPostFrameCallback` to check `isComposingRangeValid` one frame later was shared but reported as not reliable. A linked issue (#174159) was referenced for a newer related problem.
summaries.108256: Comments confirmed reproduction on macOS and Windows (desktop platforms) but not on mobile or web. The root cause was identified by a commenter: Flutter's framework uses `CharacterRange.at()` (grapheme cluster boundary) for backspace deletion after PR #115900, which deletes the entire grapheme cluster (e.g., consonant + vowel in Khmer) instead of one code point at a time. The proposed fix direction is to delete by code point rather than grapheme when there is no active composing range, and delegate to the IME only when a composing range is active. A discussion noted that on iOS, the keyboard manager decomposes precomposed Hangul syllables and deletes one jamo at a time, suggesting platform delegation is preferable. The assignee (LongCatIsLooong) was removed after no status updates; PR #115900 is cited as the introduction point.
summaries.107603: Comments confirmed that on Android, an app must declare a `<queries>` block in its manifest referencing `android.intent.action.PROCESS_TEXT` to enumerate third-party text-action providers; without it, no results are returned. A community member (@gualse) implemented a working proof-of-concept plugin. Engine PR #44579 was filed by @bleroux for the Android implementation, and a separate framework tracking issue #139361 was created. Confirmed that iOS has no equivalent of Android's `PROCESS_TEXT` API, and Jetpack Compose's `TextField` also lacks this feature. The team agreed this belongs in Flutter proper (not flutter/packages) and that the manifest `<queries>` entry should be added to the `flutter create` template. The Linux/macOS Services menu was noted as a potential analog for macOS.
summaries.79933: Comments confirmed the issue on both Windows and macOS: the IME candidate window (Sogou and similar input methods) does not track the cursor position and instead stays fixed at the lower-left corner of the screen on macOS, or below the text field at a fixed position on Windows, rather than following the insertion cursor. The bug is absent in Chrome (web). The issue is an engine-level problem requiring communication of the cursor position to the OS IME, affecting all desktop platforms. No fix or PR was identified; the issue remains open.
summaries.95923: Comments confirmed the crash is reproducible on both stable (2.8.1) and master channels when using the Telex Vietnamese IME on Flutter web (Chrome). The root cause is that the Telex IME synthesizes a key-up event for Space without a preceding key-down, causing a framework assertion failure in `HardwareKeyboard._assertEventIsRegular` (`_pressedKeys.containsKey` fails). After the assert fires, `_keyEventsSinceLastMessage` is never cleared, leaving subsequent key events unable to process. The issue is web-only — the Windows desktop target is unaffected. No fix has landed.
summaries.106475: Comments confirmed the issue occurs on Windows when a modifier key is held long enough to generate repeat events. The root cause was traced by a commenter: the engine sends a synthesized key-up before a key-repeat event (due to Windows IME interaction), so the framework receives a key-repeat for a key it believes is not pressed, hitting the `_pressedKeys.containsKey` assertion in `HardwareKeyboard._assertEventIsRegular`. After the assertion fires in debug mode, `_keyEventsSinceLastMessage` is not cleared, permanently breaking subsequent shortcut handling until app restart. The bug only manifests in debug builds (the assertion is stripped in release). No fix has been filed.
